patch.value: GH-576: pm <unknown-command> --help exits 0 with root help instead of unknown-command error — false positive for agent capability probes

pm badcmd fails correctly (non-zero, suggestions), but pm badcmd --help prints ROOT help and exits 0 — the -h/--help interception layer short-circuits unknown-command detection. Probing 'pm <cmd> --help' is the canonical way agents check whether a subcommand exists and learn its contract, so this returns a false positive: the agent concludes the command exists, misreads root help as the command's options, and burns turns on the eventual error. Same interception-layer class as pm-albl (contract layer intercepts -h/--help before variadic-positional handlers); distinct from GH-490/pm-g543 (suggestion ranking). Fix: unknown-command detection must run before (or inside) help interception — pm <unknown> --help should behave like pm <unknown>: non-zero exit, 'Unknown command <name>', did-you-mean suggestions.

pm <unknown> --help exits non-zero with the unknown-command error and suggestions (parity with pm <unknown>); known-command --help behavior unchanged; regression spec covers both the arbitrary-name and plausible-name (e.g. pm link --help) cases; GH-576 closed with evidence
